incorrects.description: Student selected 60 (added pencils and markers instead of finding difference)

steps.text: How many more means compare, not add. Look at how far apart the bars are. What's the difference between 40 and 20?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40 (identified pencils value but did not subtract)

steps.text: That's the pencils total. How many more asks for the difference. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. How much bigger is 40 than 20?

incorrects.description: Student selected 15 (compared wrong categories or miscalculated)

steps.text: The question asks about pencils and markers. Find those two bars. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. Subtract to find how many more.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Comparison overlay shows pencils bar at 40 and markers bar at 20, with difference of 20 highlighted.

steps.text: Let me show you. How many more means compare. Pencils is 40, markers is 20. I subtract to find the difference: 40 minus 20 equals 20. The answer is 20. That's what compare means—find the difference between two values.
